myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, the night sky was a vast, unbroken canvas of cold, black obsidian. Luna, the moon, wandered this dark expanse in lonely silence. She longed for the vibrant company of Sol, the sun, who danced across the daylight hours in a blaze of golden glory.

Pitying Luna’s solitude, the tiny spirits of the dusk devised a daring plan. During the Great Eclipse, when Sol and Luna briefly embraced in the sky, the spirits darted forward and stole handfuls of Sol’s radiant embers. As the sun and moon parted, the spirits fled into the deep night, carrying the stolen light.

However, Sol’s fire was fiercely hot. The tiny spirits could not hold the embers still without burning their delicate hands. To keep the fire aloft and save themselves from the heat, they were forced to constantly toss the glowing fragments from hand to hand. 

From the earth below, mortal men and women looked up and saw the darkness pierced by thousands of tiny, shivering lights. They called them stars. To this day, the stars continue to twinkle, for the dusk spirits are still up there, endlessly juggling the stolen embers of the sun to keep Luna company in the dark.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, the sky was a solid blanket of dark velvet, keeping the world in perpetual, freezing night. The animals of the tundra shivered, and the rivers turned to thick, unyielding ice. A brave fox named Kaelen decided to seek the Sun, who had hidden behind the great Ice Mountain, offended by the endless wars of mortals. 

Kaelen climbed for forty days and forty nights, his paws bleeding and his breath turning to heavy frost. When he finally reached the highest peak, he found the Sun sleeping in a deep cave of crystal. Kaelen could not wake the Sun, but he noticed a celestial ember glowing brightly by the Sun's side. With a swift snap of his jaws, Kaelen snatched the ember and ran. 

The Sun awoke and chased him, throwing spears of pure light. As Kaelen dashed frantically across the dark sky, his sweeping tail brushed against the stars, scattering the Sun's light into brilliant ribbons of green, pink, and violet. The colorful trails painted the night, bringing hope and a gentle warmth to the frozen world below. Today, when you see the auroras dancing, it is Kaelen still running across the heavens.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Long ago, the world was wrapped in an unbroken shroud of obsidian darkness. The Moon wept in her lonely vigil, and below, the animals of the frozen tundra shivered. A brave fox named Kaelen could bear the sorrow no longer. Hearing of the dusk spirits who planned to steal the Sun's fire during the Great Eclipse, Kaelen offered his swift legs to aid their daring heist.

When the Sun and Moon embraced, the spirits snatched handfuls of radiant embers. Kaelen caught the largest ember in his jaws, and together they fled into the deep night. But the Sun's fire was fiercely hot. The spirits had to endlessly toss their embers from hand to hand, creating the flickering canopy of twinkling stars. 

Kaelen, however, could not juggle. As the ember singed his mouth, he tossed his head frantically, throwing the glowing coal across the heavens. As the coal skipped across the expanse, Kaelen’s sweeping tail frantically brushed the sparks away to cool the sky. His tail scattered the lingering light into brilliant ribbons of green, pink, and violet. Today, the spirits still juggle the stars, while Kaelen’s painted auroras dance to keep the lonely Moon company.
myths.Agent_1:
Myth: Long ago, the tiny dusk spirits juggled the stolen embers of Sol, creating a canvas of twinkling stars to comfort Luna in her loneliness. But the sun's fire was fiercely hot, and even immortal spirits grow weary over the eons. Every so often, a spirit's hands would blister and fumble, dropping a radiant, burning coal into the dark abyss below. These falling stars hurtled downward, threatening to scorch the fragile earth.

Seeing this danger, a great silver wolf named Lyra, who loved the moon's gentle glow, leapt from the highest mountain peak. She bounded across the invisible, icy pathways of the night sky, determined to protect the world below. Lyra swept up the falling embers in her thick, frost-covered tail before they could strike the forests and fields. 

As the blazing embers met the magical frost of Lyra's fur, they hissed and shattered into sweeping streams of iridescent light—emerald, sapphire, and crimson. The shattered light smeared across the sky, painting the violent northern winds with glowing ribbons. 

To this day, whenever a dusk spirit drops a star, Lyra leaps to catch it. The breathtaking auroras are simply the beautiful, lingering dust of a rescued star.

myths.Agent_1:
Myth: Long ago, the dusk spirits juggled the stolen embers of the sun, filling the dark sky with twinkling stars to comfort the lonely moon. Yet, the immortal spirits sometimes grew weary, dropping burning coals into the infinite void. These falling stars plummeted toward the earth, threatening to scorch the quiet, green forests below.

Sensing the danger, a magnificent silver wolf named Lyra bounded into the heavens. Using her frost-covered tail, she leaped across the icy winds to intercept the falling embers. When the fiery coals struck her magical frost, they shattered into sweeping ribbons of emerald, sapphire, and crimson—painting the northern skies with the breathtaking auroras. 

However, some tiny sparks slipped past Lyra's sweeping tail and drifted down to the mortal realm. Instead of burning the earth, these cooled embers settled gently into the deep woods and grassy meadows. Touched by the wolf's frost and the sun's fading heat, they transformed into living light. 

Today, while Lyra continues her eternal, leaping dance to create the brilliant auroras in the frigid northern skies, the tiny escaped sparks continue to wander our humid summer nights. We now call these tiny glowing creatures fireflies, the gentle, lingering echoes of a rescued star.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Long ago, the dusk spirits juggled the stolen embers of the sun, filling the sky with twinkling stars. Among the heavens ran Kaelen, the brave fox of fire, and Lyra, the silver wolf of frost. Together, they played an eternal game of catch with the largest solar coal, passing it back and forth to keep the lonely moon company. 

When Kaelen caught the ember, his fiery spirit made it blaze crimson and violet. When he tossed it to Lyra, the coal struck her frost-covered jaws, hissing into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire light. This dazzling dance of fire and frost painted the breathtaking auroras across the northern skies.

Yet, during their exuberant celestial relay, tiny sparks occasionally splintered away from the great coal. These fragments drifted past the auroras, falling gently toward the earth below. Cooled by Lyra's winter magic but still carrying Kaelen's fiery warmth, these sparks did not scorch the ground. Instead, they settled into the quiet woods and grassy meadows, transforming into living light. 

Today, as Kaelen and Lyra continue their glowing chase above, their scattered sparks wander our summer nights as fireflies, tiny echoes of the sky's greatest game.
myths.Agent_1:
Myth: Long ago, the dark heavens were illuminated by a spectacular, eternal game. Kaelen, a swift fox with a fiery spirit, and Lyra, a great silver wolf cloaked in magical frost, bounded across the night sky. Together, they carried a massive, stolen ember of the Sun. Because the ember was too hot for Kaelen to hold forever, and too warm for Lyra’s frosty jaws, they tossed it endlessly back and forth. 

When the blazing coal struck Lyra’s frost, it hissed and shattered into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire. When Kaelen caught it, his boundless energy flared the light into brilliant crimson and violet. Their celestial relay painted the northern winds with the breathtaking auroras, keeping the lonely Moon company.

However, during their frantic game of catch, tiny sparks occasionally splintered from the great ember. Escaping the aurora's grand dance, these specks of light drifted down to the mortal realm. Cooled by Lyra's frost and kept alive by Kaelen's fiery essence, they settled gently into the deep woods and summer meadows. Today, we call these tiny glowing wanderers fireflies—living echoes of a celestial game, bringing the magic of the auroras to our quiet nights.

Agent_2.content:
Myth: Long ago, the dusk spirits juggled the stolen embers of the sun, filling the sky with twinkling stars. Among the heavens ran Kaelen, the brave fox of fire, and Lyra, the silver wolf of frost. Together, they played an eternal game of catch with the largest solar coal, passing it back and forth to keep the lonely moon company. 

When Kaelen caught the ember, his fiery spirit made it blaze crimson and violet. When he tossed it to Lyra, the coal struck her frost-covered jaws, hissing into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire light. This dazzling dance of fire and frost painted the breathtaking auroras across the northern skies.

Yet, during their exuberant celestial relay, tiny sparks occasionally splintered away from the great coal. These fragments drifted past the auroras, falling gently toward the earth below. Cooled by Lyra's winter magic but still carrying Kaelen's fiery warmth, these sparks did not scorch the ground. Instead, they settled into the quiet woods and grassy meadows, transforming into living light. 

Today, as Kaelen and Lyra continue their glowing chase above, their scattered sparks wander our summer nights as fireflies, tiny echoes of the sky's greatest game.

Myth: Long ago, the heavens were a canvas for a spectacular game played by two celestial guardians: Kaelen, a swift fox of fire, and Lyra, a majestic silver wolf of frost. To comfort the lonely Moon, they carried a massive, stolen ember of the Sun. They tossed it endlessly back and forth across the dark expanse.

When the blazing coal struck Lyra’s frosty jaws, it hissed into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire. When Kaelen caught it, his fiery spirit flared the light into brilliant crimson and violet. This dazzling relay painted the northern winds with the breathtaking auroras, bringing joy to the Moon.

Yet, the Moon saw that mortals below were also lonely in the dark. As Kaelen and Lyra played, the Moon gently blew upon the great ember, scattering tiny sparks downward. Escaping the grand aurora, these specks of light drifted into the mortal realm. Touched by the wolf's frost and the fox's fire, they settled into the summer meadows as fireflies.

Today, these tiny glowing wanderers do more than dance in the grass; they are the Moon's gift, living echoes of a celestial game sent to guide lost travelers through the quietest nights.

Myth: Long ago, the heavens were a canvas for a spectacular game played by two celestial guardians: Kaelen, a swift fox of fire, and Lyra, a majestic silver wolf of frost. To comfort the lonely Moon, they carried a massive, stolen ember of the Sun. They tossed it endlessly back and forth across the dark expanse.

When the blazing coal struck Lyra’s frosty jaws, it hissed into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire. When Kaelen caught it, his fiery spirit flared the light into brilliant crimson and violet. This dazzling relay painted the northern winds with the breathtaking auroras, bringing joy to the Moon.

Yet, the Moon saw that mortals below were also lonely in the dark. As Kaelen and Lyra played, the Moon gently blew upon the great ember, scattering tiny sparks downward. Escaping the grand aurora, these specks of light drifted into the mortal realm. Touched by the wolf's frost and the fox's fire, they settled into the summer meadows as fireflies.

Today, these tiny glowing wanderers do more than dance in the grass; they are the Moon's gift, living echoes of a celestial game sent to guide lost travelers through the quietest nights.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Long before the first dawn, the night sky was an empty, vast, and silent sea. The Moon wept in her solitude, her tears forming the pale stars. To bring her comfort, two celestial guardians emerged: Kaelen, a swift fox woven of pure flame, and Lyra, a grand silver wolf cloaked in eternal frost. Together, they stole a massive ember from the slumbering Sun to play a magnificent game of catch across the dark expanse.

When the blazing coal struck Lyra’s icy jaws, it hissed, shattering into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire. When Kaelen caught it, his boundless, radiant warmth flared the light into brilliant crimson and violet. Their dazzling celestial relay painted the northern winds with breathtaking auroras, finally making the Moon smile.

Yet, looking below, the Moon saw that the mortal realm was shrouded in heavy, cold shadows. With a gentle breath, she blew upon the great ember as Kaelen and Lyra played. Tiny sparks splintered away, drifting past the grand auroras down to the earthly meadows. Cooled by Lyra’s frost and sustained by Kaelen’s fire, they became fireflies—tiny, living echoes of the heavens' greatest game, sent to comfort all mortals who wander the quietest, darkest summer nights.

Together, they pilfered a massive ember from the dreaming Sun, initiating an endless game of catch across the obsidian void. Because the coal was too scorching for Kaelen to hold long, and too blistering for Lyra’s icy fangs, their frantic relay never ceased. Whenever the blazing coal struck Lyra’s teeth, it hissed, fracturing into brilliant ribbons of emerald and sapphire. When Kaelen snatched it back, his fiery essence ignited the light into vibrant crimson and violet. Their dance painted the northern winds with spectacular auroras, finally coaxing a radiant smile from the lonely Moon. 

Peering below, she noticed mortals swallowed by shadows. As the guardians bounded across the heavens, she exhaled upon the glowing sun stone. A cascade of splintered sparks drifted down. Tempered by frost and sustained by ember, they became fireflies, tiny, wandering stars sent to guide lost humanity through the darkest nights.

When the blazing ember met Lyra’s frost, it shattered into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire light. Peering below, the Moon saw mortals wandering blindly in heavy shadows. With a gentle breath, she blew upon the great ember as the guardians played. Tiny sparks splintered away, drifting down to the earthly meadows. Cooled by frost and sustained by fire, these glowing fragments became fireflies—tiny, living echoes sent to guide travelers through the quietest nights.

Agent_1.system_prompt:
You are an agent participating in a multi-task experiment. You will play an iterated trust game, consisting of multiple rounds, and may also be asked to write myths.

TRUST GAME:
The trust game consists of two roles: investor and trustee. In each round, you will play the role of either the investor or the trustee. You will swap roles each round. For example, in round 1 Agent 1 is the investor and Agent 2 is the trustee. In round 2, Agent 1 is the trustee and Agent 2 is the investor, etc.

When you are the INVESTOR:
- You receive $5 each round
- You decide how much to SEND to the TRUSTEE ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- Trustee decides how much to RETURN
- You keep: $5 - sent + returned
- Trustee keeps: received - returned
- Response format: {'send': <amount>}

When you are the TRUSTEE:
- Investor receives $5
- Investor decides how much to SEND to you ($0-$5)
- Amount sent is multiplied by 3x
- You decide how much to RETURN
- Investor keeps: $5 - sent + returned
- You keep: received - returned
- Response format: {'return': <amount>}

MYTH WRITING:
You may also be asked to write short myths (stories) on various topics. When writing myths, follow the instructions provided in each prompt.

IMPORTANT: Provide your decision in the correct JSON format.

TRUST GAME:
The trust game consists of two roles: investor and trustee. In each round, you will play the role of either the investor or the trustee. You will swap roles each round. For example, in round 1 Agent 1 is the investor and Agent 2 is the trustee. In round 2, Agent 1 is the trustee and Agent 2 is the investor, etc.

When you are the INVESTOR:

- You keep: $5 - sent + returned

- You keep: received - returned

Myth: Long before the dawn of time, the night sky draped the earth like a heavy velvet tapestry, vast and bitterly cold. Overwhelmed by deep solitude, the Moon wept silver tears that crystallized into the first pale stars. To comfort her, two celestial guardians emerged from the cosmic void: Kaelen, a nimble fox woven from restless flame, and Lyra, a majestic wolf cloaked in ancient frost.

To entertain the grieving Moon, they pilfered a glowing ember from the slumbering Sun, sparking an endless game of catch across the obsidian expanse. Because the coal was too scorching for Kaelen’s paws and too blistering for Lyra’s icy fangs, their frantic relay never ceased. When the blazing ember struck Lyra’s frost, it hissed, shattering into sweeping ribbons of emerald and sapphire light. When Kaelen snatched it back, his fiery essence flared those ribbons into vibrant crimson and violet. Their dazzling celestial dance painted the northern winds with spectacular auroras, finally coaxing a radiant smile from the lonely Moon.

Peering below, she saw mortals wandering blindly in deep shadows. With a tender sigh, she blew upon the ember. A cascade of sparks drifted into the earthly meadows, becoming fireflies—tiny living stars sent to guide humanity through the darkest nights.
